legacyjava/core/pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties to Java 21 to align with the JDK migration.
legacyjava/core/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 17 language features, failing to utilize or enforce Java 21 syntax and semantics required by the migration.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.general_idea: Replace String.format with String.formatted for string interpolation.
Replace String.format with String.formatted for string interpolation.
Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() introduced in Java 21.
Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() for safer empty-list handling.
legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.why_exists: List.get(0) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException on empty lists, whereas List.getFirst() throws NoSuchElementException, which is semantically correct and preferred in modern Java.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.general_idea: Replace List.get(0) with List.getFirst() to use the new Java 21 API for accessing the first element of a list.
legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.why_exists: While List.get(0) still compiles, the intent is to adopt the new Java 21 API; this is not a breaking change as the old code still runs, but it is a migration to modern syntax.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.general_idea: Replace String.format with String.formatted to use the new Java 15+ API for string formatting.
legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.why_exists: String.format still compiles and runs; this is a stylistic modernization to use the newer fluent API.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) calls with the new List.getFirst() method introduced in Java 21.
legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.why_exists: List.get(0) is not removed in Java 21, but List.getFirst() is the preferred modern API; this change is a polishment to adopt new language features rather than a fix for a compilation error.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.general_idea: Replace String.format with the new String.formatted method introduced in Java 15.
legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.why_exists: String.format is not removed in Java 21, but String.formatted is the preferred modern API; this change is a polishment to adopt new language features.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/org/javafi.general_idea: Replace String.format with the String.formatted method introduced in Java 15 for cleaner string interpolation.

legacyjava/core/src/main/java/search/Sea.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() introduced in Java 21.
legacyjava/core/src/main/java/search/Sea.why_exists: List.get(0) is not removed in Java 21, but the migration recipe explicitly targets Java 21 API modernization; however, since get(0) still compiles and runs, this is technically polishment unless the project enforces a strict ban on legacy APIs. Given the prompt's rule 'Default to polishment when in doubt' and 'breaking ONLY if ... won't compile or run', this is polishment.

legacyjava/pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties to Java 21.
legacyjava/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project compiles against Java 17, failing to utilize Java 21 features and potentially causing compatibility issues with libraries requiring Java 21.

legacyjava/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
legacyjava/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-compiler-plugin may not support compiling Java 21 source code or may fail to recognize new language features.
